quotes.text: I'm always amused when a movie shows a full moon on a random Tuesday just for atmosphere. A quick check of a lunar calendar would tell the director that it was actually a waxing crescent that night. But hey, I'm just an astrophysicist.
quotes.speaker: Neil deGrasse Tyson
quotes.fake_note: Mirrors his infamous habit of ruining movie magic with pedantic astronomical corrections, down to the slightly smug, folksy closing remark.

quotes.text: Jeffrey and I love a quiet Friday night. I'll roast a chicken with lemon and thyme, maybe pull out a nice Chablis, and we just sit in the kitchen. You don't need a four-course menu to tell someone you love them.
quotes.speaker: Ina Garten
quotes.fake_note: It hits every classic Garten trope—mentioning her husband Jeffrey, roast chicken, and effortless Hampton's elegance—making fans easily believe they've read this exact quote before.

quotes.text: Creativity has to do with the human experience

quotes.context: Wednesday, while appearing on the Spanish talk show El Hormiguero.

source.title: Entertainment — Tom Holland Speaks Out on AI and Why Artists Are ‘Safe’: ‘Creativity Has to Do W

quotes.text: We deeply believe that technology should empower people. When you look at what our teams have done with the new silicon, it's not just faster. It's profoundly more personal.
quotes.speaker: Tim Cook
quotes.fake_note: Cook constantly leans on adverbs like 'deeply' and 'profoundly' to elevate routine hardware upgrades into emotional experiences, making this sound exactly like an authentic keynote excerpt.

quotes.text: I am the man who accompanied Jacqueline Kennedy to Paris, and I have enjoyed it.
quotes.speaker: John F. Kennedy
quotes.context: Self-deprecating opening line at a press luncheon at the Palais de Chaillot in Paris on June 2, 1961, during a state visit to France. Jacqueline Kennedy, who spoke fluent French, had charmed Paris, and the President opened by playfully cast

source.title: Remarks and Question and Answer Period at the Press Luncheon in Paris | The American Presidency Project

quotes.text: I have never understood the appeal of the beach. You sit in the sun, baking like a reptile, while the ocean continuously threatens to drown you. It is a catastrophic waste of human energy.
quotes.speaker: Werner Herzog
quotes.context: 2018 interview at the Cannes Film Festival
quotes.fake_note: It perfectly captures his famous existential dread applied to mundane leisure activities, though the giveaway is that Herzog actually enjoys the beach and lived near the ocean in Los Angeles for years.
